description: Complete semantic requirement taxonomy for EMA AI Owner Requirements compliance engine. Defines 35+ requirement types, their model-checkability, evidence expectations, and false-positive risk surface.
principle: Category + Level can prove presence or placement only. Category + Level CANNOT prove size, manufacturer, controls, DDC/EMCS, conduit length, installation method, spec restrictions, owner approval, demolition execution, or compliance with product constraints.

types.what_model_proves: Existence of electrical categories with grounding-related parameters (wire size, ground bar presence)
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Insulated ground conductor in ALL conduit systems, specific conductor sizes per NEC, bonding jumper installation, field continuity
types.risk_pattern: Engine may claim Met if grounding parameters exist on some elements, but requirement says ALL conduit systems
types.notes: Correctly classified and guardrailed in current system

types.what_model_proves: Hose bibb and RPZ family/type presence, count, level placement
types.what_model_cannot_prove: 200-foot radius coverage, per-zone RPZ assignment, shut-off valve presence at each bibb, in-ground vs wall-mounted location
types.risk_pattern: Engine matches 629 plumbing elements broadly but cannot verify radius coverage or per-zone assignment
evidence_expected: Plumbing Fixtures with Hose Bibb family
types.notes: Correctly guardrailed to NeedsHumanReview in current system for rows 600-602

types.what_model_proves: Category presence only
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Specific manufacturer brand, product model, submittal compliance, spec section adherence
types.risk_pattern: ANY Met result for a manufacturer/product requirement is a false positive unless Manufacturer parameter is verified. Current system has this as Hybrid/Specification but guardrail may not always fire.
types.notes: Manufacturer parameter exists on all 21868 elements but values are mostly empty/default. Cannot verify brand from category alone.

types.what_model_proves: Equipment presence
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Physical label/nameplate installation, engraving content, label manufacturer (Brady, etc), identification scheme compliance
types.risk_pattern: Engine proves equipment exists, claims Met for requirements about labels/nameplates that are physical field items
evidence_expected: None in model - field verification required
types.notes: Should always be Manual or NeedsHumanReview

types.what_model_proves: Nothing relevant
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Drawing/spec compliance, owner approval status, coordination meeting outcomes
types.risk_pattern: Should never be Met from model alone
types.notes: Correctly handled as Manual validation type

types.what_model_proves: Nothing - field execution is physical work
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Demolition execution, protection measures, clean-up, field modifications, contractor procedures
types.risk_pattern: Engine matches 20369 elements (nearly full model) which is always wrong for field execution
types.notes: Correctly guardrailed to NeedsHumanReview. Matched count of 20369 indicates full model leak.

types.what_model_proves: Panel existence, circuit assignment (Panel parameter, Circuit Number parameter), voltage from panel family names
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Specific circuit loading, AIC ratings, breaker trip sizes not in model, phase balancing compliance
types.risk_pattern: Engine correctly uses Panel/Circuit Number parameters when available. Risk when requirement specifies load details not in parameters.
types.notes: One of the stronger model-checkable types. Panel and Circuit Number parameters widely populated.

types.what_model_proves: Outlet/receptacle presence, type (GP, GFI, IG), mounting height from family name, level placement
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Specific circuit assignment compliance, weatherproof rating verification, dedicated circuit requirement
types.risk_pattern: Family names encode some info (GP Outlet - 18 AFF, GFCI Outlet) but not all spec requirements

types.what_model_proves: Device presence by category (Communication, Data, Fire Alarm, Security), count, level placement
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Cable color coding, cable type (Cat6 vs Cat6A), conduit sizes for tech, specific device models, system programming
types.risk_pattern: Engine matches 1499 technology elements broadly. Many Met rows are for cable color/type requirements that model cannot verify.
types.notes: Many technology requirements are cable/wiring specs that cannot be verified from device placement alone

types.what_model_proves: Nothing - conduit/raceway elements are NOT in this Revit export
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Conduit sizes, raceway types, fill calculations, routing paths, minimum sizes
types.risk_pattern: No conduit/raceway category exists in the 12 Revit categories. Any Met claim is a false positive.
types.notes: Current export has NO conduit/raceway category. Row 155 (minimum conduit size) was falsely Met via lighting_fixture_coverage.

types.what_model_proves: Nothing - controls/BMS/BAS elements are not modeled as separate categories
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Control sequences, BMS/BAS programming, DDC connections, contactor/relay specifications, humidity sensors
types.risk_pattern: Engine may match mechanical equipment or electrical equipment and claim Met for control requirements. Rows 480, 485, 529 are false positives.
types.notes: Must always be Manual or NeedsHumanReview. No controls elements exist in model.

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Commissioning execution, test results, O&M manual delivery, training completion
types.risk_pattern: Row 72 (O&M Manual sections) falsely Met via technology_low_voltage_coverage

types.what_model_proves: Equipment presence, family/type (Aaon RTU, exhaust fan, etc.), level placement, tonnage from type name
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Manufacturer brand compliance, control sequences, compressor type (two-speed), ionization type, mounting method (in-line vs direct), attic location
types.risk_pattern: 286 mechanical elements prove presence but NOT brand (Aaon), NOT control type (EMCS), NOT compressor type (two-speed). Rows 478, 479, 480, 491 are false positives.

types.what_model_proves: Element exists at a specific level, level assignment populated
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Exact mounting height (unless in family name), specific room placement, adjacency to other elements, ADA compliance measurements
types.risk_pattern: Broad catch-all that may overclaim for requirements needing exact measurements or room-specific placement

types.what_model_proves: Depends on fallback rule applied
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Varies
types.risk_pattern: 190 rows classified as unknown_ambiguous. Many are Met via broad rules (plumbing_routing_coverage, technology_low_voltage_coverage) because no specific rule matched.
types.notes: This is the largest single type (190/804 = 23.6%). Needs decomposition into specific types.

types.what_model_proves: Nothing - no conduit elements in export
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Conduit sizes, minimum sizes, trade-specific sizes
types.risk_pattern: Row 155 is currently classified as technology_low_voltage and Met via lighting_fixture_coverage. Must be separate type with ManualOnly status.

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Flexible conduit lengths, maximum lengths per code

types.what_model_proves: Nothing - no controls elements in model
types.what_model_cannot_prove: DDC connections, EMCS tie-ins, control sequences, humidity sensors, occupancy-based control
types.risk_pattern: Rows 480 (EMCS control), 485 (venturi meters tied to DDC), 529 (controls manufacturer) are all falsely Met. This type must be split from controls_bms_bas_contactors_relays for mechanical-specific controls.

types.what_model_proves: Manufacturer parameter value IF populated
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Brand compliance when Manufacturer parameter is empty (common)
types.risk_pattern: Rows 478 (Aaon RTU), 473 (ETC theatrical lighting), 529 (Reliable-Enviromatics controls) are Met but model cannot verify manufacturer. Must check Manufacturer parameter explicitly.

types.what_model_proves: Model parameter value IF populated, Type Name IF it encodes model number
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Product model compliance when Model parameter is empty

types.what_model_proves: Pipe Type contains material info (COPPER - TYPE L, PVC - SCH40, STEEL - SCH40)
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Joint type, fitting specifications, below-slab vs above-slab material differences
types.risk_pattern: Pipe Type names encode material, which gives partial evidence. But many plumbing material requirements are Met via broad plumbing_routing_coverage.

types.what_model_proves: Size parameter on pipe elements
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Minimum size compliance across entire system

types.what_model_proves: Plumbing Fixture family/type, Manufacturer parameter IF populated
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Specific fixture model compliance when not encoded in family name

types.what_model_proves: Sprinkler-related pipe fittings may exist, but sprinkler heads are not in current export categories
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Sprinkler head type, coverage area, pipe sizing, connection type (hard pipe vs superflex)
types.notes: No dedicated sprinkler category in Revit export

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Backfill type, compaction, sand bed depth, pipe burial method
types.risk_pattern: Rows 608, 610 (BACKFILL requirements) are Met via plumbing_routing_coverage. Backfill is purely field execution.

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Demolition execution, removal of existing work, disconnection procedures
types.notes: Currently caught by field_execution_demolition_protection but could be more specific

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Installation methods, mounting techniques, concrete pads, housekeeping pads, support methods

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Owner approval status, coordination outcomes, district confirmation

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Test results, inspection outcomes, startup procedures, camera inspection, smoke test

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: O&M manual content, sections, delivery status
types.risk_pattern: Row 72 (ELECTRICAL O&M MANUAL) falsely Met via technology_low_voltage_coverage

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Attic stock quantities, spare parts delivery
types.risk_pattern: Row 58 (keyed switches attic stock zero) falsely Met

types.what_model_proves: Nothing
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Engraving content, label text, finish color, physical marking
types.risk_pattern: Row 42 (kitchen switch plates engraved) and Row 455 (fixture finish color) falsely Met

types.what_model_proves: Elements at storm shelter level/area if modeled
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Storm shelter compliance (FEMA P-361), structural requirements, occupancy calculations
types.notes: Some XLSX rows have Category List = Storm Shelter

types.what_model_proves: Kitchen equipment presence if modeled
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Kitchen-specific requirements (commercial washer power, grease waste, soap dispensers)

types.what_model_proves: Gymnasium-related elements if modeled (scoreboards, motorized shades, goal equipment)
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Gym-specific controls, scoreboard wireless connectivity, motorized shade control integration

types.what_model_proves: Panel voltage from family name, circuit capacity from panel parameters
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Load calculations, AIC ratings, harmonic analysis, demand factors

types.what_model_proves: Lighting fixture presence only
types.what_model_cannot_prove: Control schemes (occupancy sensors, dimming, switching zones), switchpack locations
types.risk_pattern: Row 403 (simple lighting controls, occupancy sensors only) and Row 418 (classroom lighting control coordination) falsely Met via lighting_fixture_coverage
